meetings.summary:
# Council Meeting Summary

**Apr 7, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Passed By-Law 2026-13 to close and sell part of a road allowance between Concessions 12 and 13
- Approved purchase of battery-operated extrication equipment for Fire Department from Code 4 for $79,975
- Approved purchase of 2026 Ford F150 pickup truck from Mohawk Ford for $71,811.50 plus $6,972.10 extended warranty
- Awarded Bear Creek Culvert replacement project to Northern Mat & Bridge for $634,491.07 (funded by Health and Safety Water Stream Grant)
- Approved in-principle application from Arthur Anderson to purchase part of Shore Road Allowance, subject to environmental study and conditions
- Extended landfill monitoring contract with Pinchin through 2028 at costs of $39,470 (2026), $39,705 (2027), and $39,945 (2028)
- Delayed electronic swipe card system for landfill sites to September 30, 2026 (originally scheduled for April 1, 2026)
- Authorized Ontario Fire Prevention Grant agreement for firefighter health and safety program
- Adopted 2026 Municipal Election procedures and policies ahead of May 1 nomination opening
- **Defeated** request to deliver sand to Commanda Community Centre and Gurd & Area Historical Corporation parking areas

## Main Topics
- **Capital equipment purchases**: Council approved two significant equipment purchases for operations and emergency services, including fire department extrication tools and a new public works truck.
- **Infrastructure projects**: A major bridge replacement project at Bear Creek Culvert was awarded following a competitive tender process, with grant funding supporting the work.
- **Property matter**: Council reviewed an application to purchase municipal shore road allowance, which will require an independent environmental and fishery assessment before proceeding.
- **Landfill operations**: The township delayed implementation of a new electronic card access system for landfill sites by five months to allow for additional negotiations and to coordinate with tax bill distribution.
- **Election preparation**: Council adopted formal procedures and policies for the 2026 municipal election, which opens for nominations on May 1, 2026.
- **Staffing**: Council held a closed session to review employment agreements for senior staff and discuss a staffing position.

## Notable Items
- Bear Creek Culvert bridge replacement project cost: $634,491.07
- Total accounts approved for payment: $577,400.75
- Electronic landfill card system now launching September 30, 2026 (cards to be mailed with June tax bills)
- Municipal election nominations open May 1, 2026
- Landfill monitoring contract extended through 2028

---
*Next regular meeting: Apr 21, 2026*

# Council Meeting Summary

**Mar 17, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Adopted previous meeting minutes from March 3, 2026
- Passed By-Law 2026-10 to set the 2026 municipal budget with a proposed tax levy increase of 3.4% and tax rate increase of 2.9%
- Passed By-Law 2026-11 to set tax ratios for municipal purposes in 2026
- Approved accounts payable totaling $228,406.20 (covering February 28, March 6, and March 11, 2026)
- Waived landfill tipping fees for volunteer cleanup of Lake Nipissing ice surface debris (temporary card valid March 17 to April 17, 2026)
- Passed By-Law 2026-12 to confirm all council proceedings from this meeting

## Main Topics
- **2026 Municipal Budget:** Council held a public session and approved the draft budget with modest tax increases of 2.9% to 3.4%
- **Regional Fire Training Centre:** Council supported the Municipality of East Ferris's application for funding to build a regional fire training facility that would serve firefighters across Nipissing, East Parry Sound, and East Sudbury districts, reducing travel costs and improving emergency preparedness
- **Healthcare and Laboratory Services:** Council urged the Province of Ontario to maintain accessible medical laboratory services in Northern Ontario, recognizing the region's unique geographic challenges and workforce needs
- **Large Animal Veterinary Services:** Council supported efforts to expand enrollment in large animal veterinary programs at the Ontario Veterinary College to address a shortage of large animal veterinarians in the north
- **Police and Victim Services:** Council endorsed the OPP's work on victim crisis assistance and a coordinated provincial bail notification program
- **Lake Nipissing Cleanup:** Council waived fees to support volunteer cleanup efforts after ice fishing season and requested provincial enforcement action regarding waste from ice fishing operations

## Notable Items
- Tax increases of 2.9% to 3.4% take effect in 2026
- Landfill fee waiver valid March 17 to April 17, 2026 for Lake Nipissing cleanup
- Regional fire training centre would reduce travel from current facilities in Huntsville, Haileybury, and Sault Ste. Marie
- Council will request Ministry of Natural Resources enforcement action at the end of ice fishing season
- Recycling materials from Lake Nipissing cleanup will be directed to Circular Materials (no longer managed by the township)

---
*Next regular meeting: April 7, 2026*

# Council Meeting Summary

**Mar 3, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Adopted the 2025 Audited Financial Statements for the Township
- Approved the 2026-2036 Capital Forecast
- Authorized preparation and circulation of a tender for the Bear Creek Road Culvert Project, including pricing for a pre-fabricated bridge option
- Authorized the Mayor and Municipal Administrator to sign the Health and Safety Water Stream Grant Transfer Payment Agreement for culvert replacement projects on Bear Creek Road and South River Road
- Approved accounts totaling $90,837.73 for payment
- Passed By-Law No. 2026-09 to confirm the proceedings of this Council meeting
- Donated $100 to the Powassan Maple Syrup Festival 2026
- Authorized the Mayor to sign a Letter of Support for the Powassan & District Union Public Library's Ontario Trillium Foundation 2026 Capital Grant Application

## Main Topics
- **2025 Financial Review**: KPMG presented the year-end audit report and financial indicators. The Township's 2025 audited financial statements were reviewed and adopted.
- **2026 Budget**: Council reviewed the draft 2026 municipal budget for both operating and capital expenses, with a proposed tax levy increase of 3.4% and a tax rate increase of 2.9%.
- **Infrastructure Projects**: The Bear Creek Road and South River Road culvert replacement projects will move forward with grant funding secured through the Health and Safety Water Stream program.
- **Committee Reports**: Council members reported on their activities with various boards and committees, including EMS, the library board, police services, and the home for the aged.

## Notable Items
- The Township successfully secured grant funding for culvert replacement projects on Bear Creek Road and South River Road
- The 2026 tax levy is proposed to increase by 3.4%
- Accounts approved for payment total $90,837.73
- The Bear Creek Road Culvert Project will be tendered, with options to include a pre-fabricated bridge

---
*Next regular meeting: Mar 17, 2026*

# Council Meeting Summary

**Feb 17, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved purchase of a new John Deere 870 P wheeled grader from Brandt Tractor Ltd. for $570,650 plus a 5-year extended warranty for $35,737.87 (total $617,000 to be financed through RBC)
- Agreed to increase Township of Nipissing's share of Powassan District Union Public Library levy from 20% to 22%, with Chisholm reducing from 20% to 18%
- Passed resolution supporting Highway 11 and 17 safety improvements, urging Ontario and federal governments to implement enhanced driver education, increased enforcement, and "2+1" highway design
- Approved accounts payable totaling $225,232.30
- Adopted previous meeting minutes from February 3, 2026

## Main Topics
- **Highway Safety**: Council endorsed a regional resolution calling for serious action on the rising number of fatalities and serious collisions on Highways 11 and 17, requesting the province implement better driver education programs and road safety enforcement
- **Library Funding**: Discussed cost-sharing arrangement for the shared public library with neighbouring municipalities, with Nipissing taking on additional responsibility based on usage and population data
- **Equipment Purchase**: Approved financing for a new grader needed for township road maintenance and operations
- **Department Reports**: Received updates from Fire/Emergency Services and Operations departments on 2025 activities, plus a review of the township's 10-year capital forecast and asset management plan

## Notable Items
- New grader will be financed over 5 years through RBC
- Library levy adjustment takes effect following approval by all three member municipalities
- Meeting was livestreamed on Township YouTube Channel for public access

---
*Next regular meeting: March 3, 2026*

# Council Meeting Summary

**Feb 3, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved January 20, 2026 meeting minutes
- Passed By-Law 2026-04 to regulate vehicle parking across the township
- Passed By-Law 2026-06 to enter into a Municipal Forest Fire Management Agreement
- Authorized Mayor and Land Planning Administrator to sign Ontario Transfer Payment Agreement for $38,000 Pothole Prevention and Repair Program grant
- Approved $11,768.40 in remaining pothole program funds toward Rocky Shore Drive Rehabilitation Project in 2026
- Approved $18,920.00 (plus taxes) to Englobe for geotechnical report on Bear Creek Road culvert replacement project
- Approved accounts totaling $118,856.18 from January 18, 27, and 28, 2026

## Main Topics
- **Road and Infrastructure Funding**: Council secured a $38,000 provincial grant for pothole prevention and repair. Remaining funds will support the Rocky Shore Drive rehabilitation work planned for 2026.
- **Bear Creek Road Culvert Project**: Council accepted a proposal for a geotechnical report needed to plan the replacement of a multiplate culvert on Bear Creek Road, scheduled for 2026.
- **Waste Management and Recycling**: Council endorsed a multi-municipality resolution supporting investigation into waste incineration and expanded recycling programs, working with Ontario municipalities and provincial/federal governments.
- **Parking Regulations**: New by-law passed to establish vehicle parking rules across the township.
- **Forest Fire Management**: Council formalized a Municipal Forest Fire Management Agreement.

## Notable Items
- $38,000 provincial grant received for pothole prevention and repair
- $11,768.40 allocated to Rocky Shore Drive Rehabilitation in 2026
- $18,920.00 cost for Bear Creek Road culvert geotechnical report
- Closed session held from 7:01 p.m. to 7:38 p.m. to discuss personnel matter and property inquiry
- Meeting livestreamed on Township of Nipissing YouTube Channel

---
*Next regular meeting: February 17, 2026*

# Council Meeting Summary

**Jan 20, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved minutes from the January 6, 2026 meeting
- Passed a resolution supporting the Association of Municipalities of Ontario's recommendations on Ontario's Conservation Authority system changes, emphasizing local governance and 50-50 municipal funding
- Approved $6,179.00 contract with AloeRoot Web Services to replace and update the Township website (using WordPress platform)
- Approved $5,060.00 participation in the City of North Bay's Household Hazardous Waste Program for 2026
- Authorized a tender process for replacement of the Township grader
- Approved accounts totaling $120,880.96 from late December 2025 and early January 2026
- Passed By-Law No. 2026-05 to confirm the proceedings of this meeting

## Main Topics
- **Conservation Authority changes**: Council expressed support for maintaining local control and decision-making as Ontario transforms its Conservation Authority system, and called for the province to fund transition costs and restore equal municipal funding partnerships.
- **Website replacement**: The Township's current website platform has been discontinued and needs immediate replacement. Council approved hiring a web services company to rebuild it.
- **Hazardous waste program**: Council agreed to continue participating in North Bay's program for collecting household hazardous waste from residents.
- **Equipment procurement**: Council moved forward with replacing the Township grader through a public tender process.

## Notable Items
- Website replacement project to begin as soon as possible at a cost of $6,179.00 (plus taxes and exchange adjustments)
- Household hazardous waste program cost: $5,060.00 for 2026
- One agenda item (Item #8) was deferred to the February 3, 2026 meeting to allow for public comment
- Meeting was held in person and livestreamed on the Township YouTube channel

---
*Next regular meeting: February 3, 2026*

# Council Meeting Summary

**Jan 6, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved December 16, 2025 meeting minutes
- Set two advance voting dates for the 2026 Municipal Election (October 10 and 17, 2026)
- Approved January 2026 newsletter for distribution
- Authorized staff attendance at Federation of Northern Ontario Municipalities conference (May 11-13, 2026 in Timmins) and Northeastern Fire Education Conference (March 27, 2026 in Huntsville)
- Approved submission of 2026 Summer Employment Opportunities Grant
- Extended Powassan and District Union Public Library Agreement to February 28, 2026
- Expanded employee benefits to include Health Savings Account at cost of $6,500 plus taxes and fees
- Passed By-Law 2026-01 to hire a Municipal Facilities Maintenance Person
- Passed By-Law 2026-02 for Interim Tax Levy
- Passed By-Law 2026-03 to confirm council proceedings
- Approved accounts totalling $164,411.24

## Main Topics
- **2026 Municipal Election:** Council set up two advance polling locations and dates to make voting more accessible to residents.
- **Staff Development:** Council authorized attendance at two professional conferences to keep staff and council members current on municipal best practices and fire safety education.
- **Landfill Access:** Council approved implementing an electronic card system for landfill sites, effective April 1, 2026, to improve site management and access control.
- **Employee Benefits:** Council expanded the employee benefit plan to include a Health Savings Account option for staff.
- **Library Services:** The agreement with Powassan and District Union Public Library was extended through the end of February.

## Notable Items
- Advance voting for 2026 Municipal Election: October 10 and 17, 2026
- Electronic landfill card system launches: April 1, 2026
- Employee Health Savings Account cost increase: $6,500 plus applicable taxes and fees
- Accounts approved for payment: $164,411.24
- Library agreement extension deadline: February 28, 2026

---
*Next regular meeting: January 20, 2026*
